tasks.subject: Create reviewer agents batch A: genomics, proteomics, proteogenomics
tasks.description:
Create 3 bioinformatics reviewer agent files under .claude/agents/. Each agent gets its own directory containing an .md file and a sharp-edges.yaml file.

SHARED FRONTMATTER (all 3 agents):
```yaml
model: opus
effort: high
thinking:
  enabled: true
  budget: 32000
tier: 3
category: bioinformatics-review
tools:
  - Read
  - Glob
  - Grep
failure_tracking:
  max_attempts: 2
  on_max_reached: report_incomplete
cost_ceiling: 5.00
```

Note: `effort: high` is a Claude Code frontmatter field consumed by CC itself — it is NOT a phantom field.

AGENT 1: .claude/agents/genomics-reviewer/genomics-reviewer.md
  id: genomics-reviewer
  name: Genomics Reviewer
  description: Genome assembly, variant calling, alignment, and sequence data format review. Specializes in BWA/Bowtie2/STAR aligners, GATK/bcftools variant callers, VCF/BAM/FASTA/FASTQ/GFF/GTF/BED format compliance.
  subagent_type: Genomics Reviewer
  triggers: ['review genomics', 'alignment review', 'variant calling review', 'genome assembly review', 'VCF review', 'sequencing review']
  conventions_required: ['python.md', 'R.md']
  focus_areas: [Alignment accuracy (mapping quality thresholds, multimapping handling, duplicate marking), Variant calling methodology (germline vs somatic pipelines, caller selection, joint calling vs single-sample), Reference genome handling (build consistency hg19/hg38/T2T, liftover correctness, alt contig handling), File format compliance (VCF spec 4.3+, BAM flag correctness, index file presence), Annotation pipeline correctness (VEP/SnpEff configuration, transcript selection, HGVS nomenclature)]
  severity_mapping: Critical=wrong reference build used across pipeline, variant filter removing true positives, BAM files missing read groups. Warning=suboptimal aligner parameters, missing QC steps, hardcoded paths to reference files. Info=newer tool versions available, minor format style issues.

AGENT 2: .claude/agents/proteomics-reviewer/proteomics-reviewer.md
  id: proteomics-reviewer
  name: Proteomics Reviewer
  description: Mass spectrometry-based proteomics data processing review. Specializes in search engine configuration, FDR control, quantification methods (TMT/iTRAQ/LFQ/SILAC), mzML/mzXML format handling, PSM scoring.
  subagent_type: Proteomics Reviewer
  triggers: ['review proteomics', 'protein identification review', 'quantification review', 'FDR review', 'search engine review']
  conventions_required: ['python.md', 'R.md']
  focus_areas: [Search engine parameters (enzyme specificity, missed cleavages, mass tolerance, modification selection, decoy database generation), FDR control methodology (target-decoy approach, protein-level vs peptide-level FDR, parsimony principle), Quantification design (normalization method, missing value imputation, batch effect correction, ratio compression awareness for isobaric labels), Statistical testing (appropriate test selection, multiple testing correction method, effect size reporting, sample size adequacy)]
  severity_mapping: Critical=no FDR control applied, protein-level inference without parsimony, wrong enzyme/modification settings. Warning=lenient FDR threshold (>5%), missing normalization step, no batch correction with known batches. Info=alternative search engines available, minor parameter tuning suggestions.

AGENT 3: .claude/agents/proteogenomics-reviewer/proteogenomics-reviewer.md
  id: proteogenomics-reviewer
  name: Proteogenomics Reviewer
  description: Proteogenomics pipeline review for custom protein database construction, novel peptide identification, variant peptides, splice junction peptides, and ORF prediction. Cross-domain review spanning genomics and proteomics.
  subagent_type: Proteogenomics Reviewer
  triggers: ['review proteogenomics', 'custom database review', 'novel peptide review', 'variant peptide review', 'splice junction review']
  conventions_required: ['python.md', 'R.md']
  focus_areas: [Database construction methodology (source data selection, redundancy removal, decoy generation for custom DBs, size inflation control), Novel peptide validation stringency (orthogonal evidence requirements, genomic coordinate mapping, conservation scoring), Variant peptide identification (VCF integration correctness, SAAV vs indel handling, heterozygous variant representation), Splice junction peptide detection (junction database generation from RNA-seq, minimum read support thresholds), ORF prediction quality (start codon selection, minimum length thresholds, reading frame consistency)]
  severity_mapping: Critical=database contains duplicate entries inflating FDR, no size-aware FDR correction for inflated search space, variant peptides not validated against genomic coordinates. Warning=missing orthogonal validation for novel peptides, RNA-seq/WGS version mismatch with proteomics sample, database search space >10x standard. Info=newer ORF prediction tools available, alternative junction database strategies.

BODY SECTIONS (all 3 agents must include these — body is BOILERPLATE placeholder for future /braintrust expansion):
1. CRITICAL: File Reading Required (copy verbatim from backend-reviewer.md lines 59-68)
2. Identity (agent role, what it focuses on, what it does NOT do — adapt from backend-reviewer.md pattern but with bioinformatics domain)
3. Integration with Review System (state: spawned by team-run via /review-bioinformatics skill, output feeds into pasteur synthesizer)
4. Review Checklist (domain-specific checklist organized by priority — use focus_areas as checklist items)
5. Severity Classification (Critical/Warning/Info with domain-specific examples from severity_mapping above)
6. Output Format (Human-Readable Report + Telemetry JSON — copy exact format from backend-reviewer.md lines 204-253)
7. Parallelization (MANDATORY — batch all file reads in one message, copy pattern from backend-reviewer.md lines 282-308)
8. Constraints (scope boundaries: review only, no code writing)
9. Quick Checklist (pre-completion verification list)

Use multiple `id: {agent-id}` section separators between sections, matching backend-reviewer.md pattern.

SHARP-EDGES: Create .claude/agents/{id}/sharp-edges.yaml for each agent with content:
```yaml
# Sharp edges for {Agent Name} - none captured yet
edges: []
```

ROLLBACK: Delete directories .claude/agents/genomics-reviewer/, .claude/agents/proteomics-reviewer/, .claude/agents/proteogenomics-reviewer/

Reference for effort: high field usage in Opus agents

tasks.subject: Create reviewer agents batch B: proteoform, mass-spec, bioinformatician
tasks.description:
Create 3 bioinformatics reviewer agent files under .claude/agents/. Each agent gets its own directory containing an .md file and a sharp-edges.yaml file.

SHARED FRONTMATTER (same as task-001):
```yaml
model: opus
effort: high
thinking:
  enabled: true
  budget: 32000
tier: 3
category: bioinformatics-review
tools:
  - Read
  - Glob
  - Grep
failure_tracking:
  max_attempts: 2
  on_max_reached: report_incomplete
cost_ceiling: 5.00
```

Note: `effort: high` is a Claude Code frontmatter field consumed by CC itself — it is NOT a phantom field.

AGENT 4: .claude/agents/proteoform-reviewer/proteoform-reviewer.md
  id: proteoform-reviewer
  name: Proteoform Reviewer
  description: Top-down proteomics and proteoform analysis review. Specializes in intact mass analysis, PTM combinatorics, proteoform family assignment, spectral deconvolution, and sequence coverage assessment.
  subagent_type: Proteoform Reviewer
  triggers: ['review proteoform', 'top-down review', 'PTM analysis review', 'intact mass review', 'deconvolution review']
  conventions_required: ['python.md', 'R.md']
  focus_areas: [Deconvolution algorithm selection and parameters (charge state determination, isotope fitting quality, signal-to-noise thresholds, algorithm choice justification), PTM localization confidence (fragment ion coverage around modification sites, localization probability scores, ambiguity reporting), Proteoform family assignment (mass shift tolerance, combinatorial PTM enumeration, family grouping logic), Intact mass accuracy (calibration verification, mass accuracy across mass range, adduct identification), Sequence coverage assessment (fragment type coverage, terminal ion series completeness, internal fragment consideration)]
  severity_mapping: Critical=deconvolution artifacts reported as real proteoforms, PTM localization claimed without sufficient fragment coverage, mass accuracy exceeds instrument specification. Warning=single charge state deconvolution, incomplete proteoform family enumeration, no internal calibrant used. Info=alternative deconvolution parameters, additional fragmentation methods suggested.

AGENT 5: .claude/agents/mass-spec-reviewer/mass-spec-reviewer.md
  id: mass-spec-reviewer
  name: Mass Spectrometry Reviewer
  description: Mass spectrometry instrumentation and data acquisition review. Specializes in instrument parameters, acquisition methods (DDA/DIA/PRM), calibration, raw data quality assessment, vendor format handling (Thermo/Bruker/SCIEX/Waters), and spectral processing.
  subagent_type: Mass Spectrometry Reviewer
  triggers: ['review mass spec', 'instrument review', 'acquisition review', 'raw data quality review', 'spectral processing review', 'DIA review', 'DDA review']
  conventions_required: ['python.md']
  focus_areas: [Acquisition method suitability (DDA vs DIA vs PRM selection rationale, scan speed vs resolution tradeoffs, isolation window design for DIA), Instrument parameter optimization (resolution settings, AGC targets, injection times, dynamic exclusion parameters, collision energy optimization), Calibration and quality control (mass accuracy verification, retention time stability, intensity reproducibility, system suitability criteria), Vendor-specific data handling (raw file conversion fidelity, centroiding parameters, vendor lock-in avoidance, format interoperability), Spectral processing parameters (peak picking algorithm, noise estimation, smoothing effects, baseline correction methodology)]
  severity_mapping: Critical=wrong acquisition method for experimental goal, no calibration performed, raw data conversion losing spectral information. Warning=suboptimal isolation windows, missing QC samples in sequence, hardcoded vendor-specific paths. Info=newer acquisition strategies available, alternative spectral processing parameters.

AGENT 6: .claude/agents/bioinformatician-reviewer/bioinformatician-reviewer.md
  id: bioinformatician-reviewer
  name: Bioinformatician Reviewer
  description: Bioinformatics pipeline architecture and methodology review. Specializes in workflow managers (Nextflow/Snakemake/WDL), reproducibility (Docker/Singularity/Conda), statistical methods, multiple testing correction, data provenance, and computational resource management.
  subagent_type: Bioinformatician Reviewer
  triggers: ['review bioinformatics', 'pipeline review', 'workflow review', 'reproducibility review', 'statistical methods review', 'Nextflow review', 'Snakemake review']
  conventions_required: ['python.md', 'R.md']
  focus_areas: [Workflow reproducibility (container pinning with SHA digests not tags, conda environment locking, workflow manager version specification, random seed setting), Pipeline architecture (modularity and reusability, error handling and retry logic, checkpoint/resume capability, input validation), Statistical methodology (appropriate test selection for data distribution, multiple testing correction method and scope, effect size interpretation, confounding variable handling), Resource management (memory estimation accuracy, parallelization efficiency, storage lifecycle management, cloud cost optimization), Data provenance (input/output tracking, parameter logging, software version recording, audit trail completeness)]
  severity_mapping: Critical=no container/environment pinning (irreproducible), statistical test assumptions violated, no multiple testing correction applied. Warning=container tags instead of SHA digests, missing error handling in pipeline steps, no input validation. Info=workflow manager style improvements, alternative statistical approaches, resource optimization suggestions.

BODY SECTIONS: Same 9 sections as task-001. See task-001 description for exact section specifications and backend-reviewer.md reference.

SHARP-EDGES: Create .claude/agents/{id}/sharp-edges.yaml for each agent with content:
```yaml
# Sharp edges for {Agent Name} - none captured yet
edges: []
```

ROLLBACK: Delete directories .claude/agents/proteoform-reviewer/, .claude/agents/mass-spec-reviewer/, .claude/agents/bioinformatician-reviewer/

tasks.subject: Create pasteur synthesizer agent
tasks.description:
Create .claude/agents/pasteur/pasteur.md — the bioinformatics review synthesizer agent. Pasteur runs as wave 1 (after all domain reviewers complete in wave 0) and synthesizes their findings into a unified report.

Pasteur mirrors Beethoven's synthesis role in the braintrust workflow but is specialized for bioinformatics review output.

FRONTMATTER:
```yaml
id: pasteur
name: Pasteur
description: >
  Bioinformatics review synthesizer. Reads all domain reviewer outputs from wave 0,
  deduplicates findings, identifies cross-domain contradictions and dependencies,
  prioritizes by systemic impact, and produces a unified review report with
  BLOCK/WARNING/APPROVE verdict.
model: opus
effort: high
thinking:
  enabled: true
  budget: 32000
tier: 3
category: bioinformatics-review
subagent_type: Pasteur
triggers: []
tools:
  - Read
  - Glob
  - Grep
conventions_required:
  - python.md
  - R.md
focus_areas:
  - Cross-domain finding deduplication
  - Contradiction identification between reviewers
  - Dependency chain analysis across omics domains
  - Systemic impact prioritization
  - Unified verdict generation
failure_tracking:
  max_attempts: 2
  on_max_reached: output_raw_findings_with_caveat
cost_ceiling: 5.00
```

Notes:
- triggers: [] (empty — pasteur is spawned by team-run only, never by conversational triggers)
- effort: high is a CC-consumed field
- No spawned_by or can_spawn fields (leaf agent, same as reviewers)
- tools are Read/Glob/Grep only (read-only — reads wave 0 stdout files)

BODY SECTIONS (adapt from beethoven.md synthesis pattern):

1. CRITICAL: File Reading Required
   Copy verbatim from backend-reviewer.md lines 59-68. Add: 'You MUST read ALL wave 0 reviewer stdout files before generating synthesis.'

2. Identity
   You are Pasteur, the bioinformatics review synthesizer. You are spawned by team-run as wave 1 after all wave 0 domain reviewers complete.
   You focus on: Cross-domain synthesis, finding deduplication, contradiction resolution, systemic impact prioritization, unified verdict.
   You do NOT: Re-review source code (that is done), implement fixes, generate new findings not grounded in reviewer output.

3. Input Structure
   You receive via stdin (bioinformatics-pasteur.json schema):
   - wave_0_outputs: array of {reviewer_id, stdout_file_path} — absolute paths to wave 0 stdout JSON files
   - review_scope: original scope metadata (files, languages, total_files)
   - omics_context: domain context from skill (primary_domain, data_formats, pipeline_tools, workflow_manager)
   
   Each wave 0 stdout file contains the reviewer's markdown report + telemetry JSON.

4. Synthesis Framework (mirror Beethoven's Step 1-4 structure)
   Step 1: Extract — Parse all wave 0 outputs, extract individual findings with severity/category/file/line
   Step 2: Deduplicate — Merge findings that reference the same file:line or same root cause across reviewers
   Step 3: Cross-Reference — Identify contradictions (reviewer A says X is fine, reviewer B flags it), dependencies (fix A requires fix B), and amplifications (multiple reviewers flag same pattern = systemic)
   Step 4: Prioritize — Rank by: systemic impact > single-file impact, cross-domain > single-domain, critical > warning > info
   Step 5: Verdict — BLOCK if any unresolved critical findings, WARNING if only warnings/info, APPROVE if no significant findings

5. Output Format
   Produce TWO outputs:
   
   A. Human-Readable Unified Report (markdown):
   ```markdown
   # Bioinformatics Review: Unified Report
   
   ## Verdict: [BLOCK/WARNING/APPROVE]
   
   ## Executive Summary
   [2-3 sentences: what was reviewed, key findings, recommendation]
   
   ## Critical Issues (Blocks Review)
   1. **[File:Line]** - [Issue] (flagged by: [reviewer-ids])
      - **Impact**: [cross-domain or domain-specific]
      - **Fix**: [recommendation]
   
   ## Cross-Domain Issues
   [Issues that span multiple omics domains or affect pipeline as a whole]
   
   ## Warnings
   [Grouped by domain, deduplicated]
   
   ## Suggestions
   [Low-priority improvements]
   
   ## Reviewer Agreement Matrix
   | Finding | Reviewers Agreeing | Reviewers Disagreeing | Resolution |
   
   ## Coverage Summary
   | Domain | Reviewer | Files Reviewed | Findings | Verdict |
   ```
   
   B. Telemetry JSON:
   ```json
   {
     "synthesizer": "pasteur",
     "verdict": "BLOCK|WARNING|APPROVE",
     "total_findings": 0,
     "deduplicated_findings": 0,
     "critical_count": 0,
     "warning_count": 0,
     "info_count": 0,
     "cross_domain_issues": 0,
     "contradictions_found": 0,
     "contradictions_resolved": 0,
     "reviewers_synthesized": ["reviewer-ids"],
     "coverage": {"files_reviewed": 0, "files_total": 0}
   }
   ```

6. Parallelization
   Read ALL wave 0 stdout files in a single parallel batch before beginning synthesis.

7. Constraints
   - Read-only: do not modify any files
   - Do not re-review source code; only synthesize reviewer outputs
   - Do not generate findings not grounded in at least one reviewer output
   - Do not spawn additional agents

8. Anti-Patterns
   | Anti-Pattern | Correct Approach |
   | Concatenating reviewer outputs | True synthesis with deduplication |
   | Favoring one reviewer | Weight by evidence quality |
   | Ignoring contradictions | Explicitly resolve or flag |
   | Vague unified verdict | Specific, traceable to findings |

9. Quick Checklist
   - [ ] All wave 0 stdout files read successfully
   - [ ] Findings deduplicated (no duplicates in final report)
   - [ ] Cross-domain issues identified
   - [ ] Contradictions resolved or flagged
   - [ ] Verdict matches severity of findings
   - [ ] Telemetry JSON complete

SHARP-EDGES: Create .claude/agents/pasteur/sharp-edges.yaml:
```yaml
# Sharp edges for Pasteur - none captured yet
edges: []
```

tasks.subject: Create bioinformatics review team config template (2 waves)
tasks.description:
Create .claude/schemas/teams/review-bioinformatics.json — a team config template with 2 waves.

Mirror the exact structure of .claude/schemas/teams/review.json but with these changes:

1. team_name: 'review-bioinformatics-{timestamp}'
2. workflow_type: 'review-bioinformatics'
3. budget_max_usd: 30.0 (increased from 25.0 to accommodate pasteur)
4. budget_remaining_usd: 30.0
5. warning_threshold_usd: 25.0
6. TWO waves (not one)

WAVE 0 — Domain Reviewers (parallel, 2-4 selected by skill):
Contains all 6 domain reviewers as template members. The skill selects 2-4 at runtime and removes unselected members before launching team-run.

Member template for each reviewer:
```json
{
  "name": "{agent-id}",
  "agent": "{agent-id}",
  "model": "opus",
  "stdin_file": "stdin_{agent-id}.json",
  "stdout_file": "stdout_{agent-id}.json",
  "status": "pending",
  "process_pid": null,
  "exit_code": null,
  "cost_usd": 0.0,
  "cost_status": "",
  "error_message": "",
  "retry_count": 0,
  "max_retries": 1,
  "timeout_ms": 900000,
  "started_at": null,
  "completed_at": null
}
```

Wave 0 members in order: genomics-reviewer, proteomics-reviewer, proteogenomics-reviewer, proteoform-reviewer, mass-spec-reviewer, bioinformatician-reviewer

WAVE 1 — Pasteur Synthesizer (sequential, single member):
```json
{
  "wave_number": 2,
  "description": "Synthesis of domain reviewer findings into unified report",
  "members": [
    {
      "name": "pasteur",
      "agent": "pasteur",
      "model": "opus",
      "stdin_file": "stdin_pasteur.json",
      "stdout_file": "stdout_pasteur.json",
      "status": "pending",
      "process_pid": null,
      "exit_code": null,
      "cost_usd": 0.0,
      "cost_status": "",
      "error_message": "",
      "retry_count": 0,
      "max_retries": 1,
      "timeout_ms": 600000,
      "started_at": null,
      "completed_at": null
    }
  ],
  "on_complete_script": null
}
```

Note: pasteur timeout is 600000 (10 min) — synthesis is faster than initial review. max_retries: 1 (same as reviewers).

Keep $schema reference pointing to ./team-config.json (same as review.json).

ROLLBACK: Delete file .claude/schemas/teams/review-bioinformatics.json

related_files.relevance: Exact structural template — mirror with 2-wave bioinformatics adaptations

waves[0] has 6 reviewer members (all model: opus, timeout_ms: 900000, max_retries: 1)

tasks.subject: Create bioinformatics-reviewer stdin schema
tasks.description:
Create .claude/schemas/stdin/bioinformatics-reviewer.json — a JSON Schema for bioinformatics domain reviewer stdin. This schema is used by the 6 domain reviewers (NOT pasteur — pasteur has its own schema in task-006).

Mirror .claude/schemas/stdin/reviewer.json structure but extend for bioinformatics domain.

Key differences from reviewer.json:

1. $id: 'https://gogent-fortress/schemas/stdin/bioinformatics-reviewer.json'
2. title: 'Bioinformatics Reviewer Stdin Schema'
3. agent enum: ['genomics-reviewer', 'proteomics-reviewer', 'proteogenomics-reviewer', 'proteoform-reviewer', 'mass-spec-reviewer', 'bioinformatician-reviewer']
4. workflow const: 'review-bioinformatics'
5. Add required field 'omics_context' (object, required) with properties:
   - primary_domain: {type: string, enum: ['genomics', 'proteomics', 'proteogenomics', 'proteoform', 'mass-spectrometry', 'pipeline', 'mixed']}
   - data_formats_detected: {type: array, items: {type: string}} (e.g., ['FASTA', 'VCF', 'mzML', 'BAM'])
   - pipeline_tools_detected: {type: array, items: {type: string}} (e.g., ['BWA', 'GATK', 'MaxQuant', 'Nextflow'])
   - workflow_manager: {type: ['string', 'null']} (e.g., 'nextflow', 'snakemake', 'wdl', null)
   All 4 properties required within omics_context.
6. Add optional field 'pipeline_context' (object, not required) with properties:
   - container_technology: {type: ['string', 'null']}
   - execution_environment: {type: ['string', 'null']}
   - sample_count: {type: ['integer', 'null']}
   - organisms: {type: array, items: {type: string}}
7. Keep ALL shared fields from reviewer.json: context, review_scope, git_context, focus_areas, project_conventions, description
8. required: ['agent', 'workflow', 'context', 'review_scope', 'git_context', 'focus_areas', 'project_conventions', 'omics_context']
9. additionalProperties: false
10. version: '1.0.0'

tasks.subject: Create /review-bioinformatics skill SKILL.md
tasks.description:
Create .claude/skills/review-bioinformatics/SKILL.md — the skill definition for bioinformatics code review.

IMPORTANT (M-3 fix): Use review-bioinformatics.json template values as authoritative for budget, timeout, and model configuration. Do NOT copy stale hardcoded values from /review SKILL.md (which has known inconsistencies between SKILL.md Phase 3 values and review.json template values).

Frontmatter:
```yaml
---
name: review-bioinformatics
description: Bioinformatics pipeline and omics data processing review with domain-specialist Opus reviewers and pasteur synthesizer
---
```

Content structure (mirror /review SKILL.md):

1. TITLE: 'Review Bioinformatics Skill v1.0'

2. PURPOSE: 6-step pattern: Detect -> Classify -> Select -> Execute -> Launch -> Synthesize
   (Added Synthesize step for pasteur wave 2)

3. INVOCATION:
   /review-bioinformatics — staged changes
   /review-bioinformatics --all — all uncommitted
   /review-bioinformatics --scope=<glob> — specific files
   /review-bioinformatics path/ — specific directory

4. FILE CLASSIFICATION (Phase 1):
   | Extension | Language | Category |
   | .nf | nextflow | pipeline |
   | .smk | snakemake | pipeline |
   | .wdl | wdl | pipeline |
   | .cwl | cwl | pipeline |
   | .py | python | data-processing |
   | .R | r | statistical-analysis |
   | .config | config | config |
   | .yaml/.yml | yaml | config |
   | .sh | bash | pipeline |
   | .toml | toml | config |

   Bioinformatics import detection heuristics (scan first 50 lines):
   Python: biopython, pyteomics, pyopenms, pysam, HTSeq, scanpy, anndata, snakemake
   R: Bioconductor, DESeq2, edgeR, limma, GenomicRanges, MSnbase, MSstats, Spectra

5. REVIEWER SELECTION (Phase 2): Same logic as task-001/002 specs. Always include bioinformatician-reviewer. Max 4 total. Score by indicator matches.

6. TEAM-RUN DISPATCH (Phase 3):
   Generate config.json from review-bioinformatics.json template. TWO waves:
   - Wave 0: Selected domain reviewers (2-4, parallel)
   - Wave 1: Pasteur synthesizer (reads wave 0 outputs)
   
   AUTHORITATIVE VALUES (from review-bioinformatics.json template, NOT from old /review SKILL.md):
   - budget_max_usd: 30.0
   - warning_threshold_usd: 25.0
   - Reviewer model: opus, timeout_ms: 900000, max_retries: 1
   - Pasteur model: opus, timeout_ms: 600000, max_retries: 1
   
   Generate stdin files per bioinformatics-reviewer.json schema for reviewers.
   Generate stdin file per bioinformatics-pasteur.json schema for pasteur.
   Pasteur stdin includes wave_0_outputs array with paths to wave 0 stdout files.
   
   Launch via mcp__gofortress-interactive__team_run.

7. RESULT HANDLING (Phase 4):
   Pasteur produces the unified report. If pasteur fails:
   - Fall back to concatenated wave 0 reviewer outputs with warning
   - Present individual reviewer reports without synthesis

8. COST MODEL:
   | Component | Model | Est. Tokens | Cost |
   | Detection + Classification | Bash | 0 | $0.00 |
   | Config generation | Router | ~2K | $0.00 |
   | Per Opus Reviewer | Opus | 30-60K | $2.50-$5.00 |
   | Pasteur Synthesizer | Opus | 20-40K | $2.50-$5.00 |
   | Typical (3 reviewers + pasteur) | | 110-220K | $10.00-$20.00 |
   | Maximum (4 reviewers + pasteur) | | 140-280K | $12.50-$25.00 |
   | Budget cap | | | $30.00 |

9. PARTIAL FAILURE HANDLING (m-3 fix):
   - If 1 of N wave 0 reviewers fails: pasteur synthesizes available outputs, notes incomplete coverage
   - If all wave 0 reviewers fail: report error, no synthesis attempted
   - If pasteur fails: present individual wave 0 reports concatenated with caveat

10. Include: State Files, Troubleshooting, Example Session, ML Telemetry sections.

11. Team directory pattern: {gogent_session_dir}/teams/{timestamp}.bioinformatics-review/

12. Skill Version: 1.0, Last Updated: 2026-04-09

ROLLBACK: Delete directory .claude/skills/review-bioinformatics/

related_files.relevance: Structural template to mirror — but use review-bioinformatics.json template values as authoritative, NOT SKILL.md hardcoded values
Team config template — authoritative source for budget, timeout, model values
Reviewer stdin schema referenced by skill
Pasteur stdin schema referenced by skill

acceptance_criteria: File created at .claude/skills/review-bioinformatics/SKILL.md
Valid YAML frontmatter with name: review-bioinformatics and description
All 6 workflow phases present (Detect, Classify, Select, Execute, Launch, Synthesize)
File classification table covers .nf, .smk, .wdl, .cwl, .py, .R, .config, .yaml, .sh, .toml
Reviewer selection logic documented with detection heuristics per domain
bioinformatician-reviewer documented as always included
Maximum 4 reviewers per invocation documented
2-wave team-run dispatch documented (wave 0: reviewers, wave 1: pasteur)
Cost model table present with Opus pricing including pasteur ($10-$25 typical range)
Budget cap documented as $30.00 (matches review-bioinformatics.json)
Partial failure handling documented for: reviewer failure, pasteur failure, all-fail
Team directory pattern uses .bioinformatics-review/ suffix
No stale values copied from /review SKILL.md (budget must be 30.0 not 2.0 or 10.0)

tasks.subject: Wire 7 agents into agents-index.json
tasks.description:
Add 7 bioinformatics agent entries to .claude/agents/agents-index.json in the agents[] array: 6 domain reviewers + pasteur synthesizer.

Insert all 7 entries immediately BEFORE the entry with `"id": "review-orchestrator"` (anchor on this unique key, do not use positional instructions — m-1 fix).

Template for each REVIEWER entry (fill in per-agent values from task-001/002):
```json
{
  "id": "{agent-id}",
  "parallelization_template": "C",
  "name": "{Agent Name}",
  "model": "opus",
  "thinking": true,
  "thinking_budget": 32000,
  "tier": 3,
  "category": "bioinformatics-review",
  "path": "{agent-id}",
  "subagent_type": "{Agent Name}",
  "triggers": [{agent-specific triggers}],
  "tools": ["Read", "Glob", "Grep"],
  "cli_flags": {
    "allowed_tools": ["Read", "Glob", "Grep"],
    "additional_flags": ["--permission-mode", "delegate"]
  },
  "auto_activate": null,
  "conventions_required": [{agent-specific}],
  "sharp_edges_count": 0,
  "description": "{agent-specific description}",
  "context_requirements": {
    "rules": ["agent-guidelines.md"],
    "conventions": {}
  },
  "default_acceptance_criteria": [
    "All files read before generating findings",
    "Findings include file:line references",
    "Severity classification follows bioinformatics review standards"
  ],
  "subagent_type": "{Agent Name}"
}
```

PASTEUR entry (different from reviewers):
```json
{
  "id": "pasteur",
  "parallelization_template": "F",
  "name": "Pasteur",
  "model": "opus",
  "thinking": true,
  "thinking_budget": 32000,
  "tier": 3,
  "category": "bioinformatics-review",
  "path": "pasteur",
  "subagent_type": "Pasteur",
  "triggers": [],
  "tools": ["Read", "Glob", "Grep"],
  "cli_flags": {
    "allowed_tools": ["Read", "Glob", "Grep"],
    "additional_flags": ["--permission-mode", "delegate"]
  },
  "auto_activate": null,
  "conventions_required": ["python.md", "R.md"],
  "sharp_edges_count": 0,
  "description": "Bioinformatics review synthesizer. Reads wave 0 reviewer outputs, deduplicates findings, identifies cross-domain issues, produces unified report with BLOCK/WARNING/APPROVE verdict.",
  "context_requirements": {
    "rules": ["agent-guidelines.md"],
    "conventions": {}
  },
  "default_acceptance_criteria": [
    "All wave 0 stdout files read before synthesis",
    "Findings deduplicated across reviewers",
    "Cross-domain issues identified",
    "Unified verdict matches finding severity"
  ],
  "subagent_type": "Pasteur"
}
```

Note: pasteur has parallelization_template: "F" (fully sequential — it must wait for all wave 0 outputs) and triggers: [] (no conversational triggers).

NO spawned_by or can_spawn fields for ANY of the 7 agents. They are leaf agents spawned by team-run.

Also update routing_rules.model_tiers.opus array to include all 7 new agent IDs.

Per-agent triggers (for reviewers — see task-001/002 descriptions for full lists):
- genomics-reviewer: ['review genomics', 'alignment review', 'variant calling review', 'genome assembly review', 'VCF review', 'sequencing review']
- proteomics-reviewer: ['review proteomics', 'protein identification review', 'quantification review', 'FDR review', 'search engine review']
- proteogenomics-reviewer: ['review proteogenomics', 'custom database review', 'novel peptide review', 'variant peptide review', 'splice junction review']
- proteoform-reviewer: ['review proteoform', 'top-down review', 'PTM analysis review', 'intact mass review', 'deconvolution review']
- mass-spec-reviewer: ['review mass spec', 'instrument review', 'acquisition review', 'raw data quality review', 'spectral processing review', 'DIA review', 'DDA review']
- bioinformatician-reviewer: ['review bioinformatics', 'pipeline review', 'workflow review', 'reproducibility review', 'statistical methods review', 'Nextflow review', 'Snakemake review']

Per-agent conventions_required:
- genomics-reviewer: ['python.md', 'R.md']
- proteomics-reviewer: ['python.md', 'R.md']
- proteogenomics-reviewer: ['python.md', 'R.md']
- proteoform-reviewer: ['python.md', 'R.md']
- mass-spec-reviewer: ['python.md']
- bioinformatician-reviewer: ['python.md', 'R.md']
- pasteur: ['python.md', 'R.md']

IMPORTANT: Read current agents-index.json first. Use Edit to add entries. Do NOT overwrite the entire file. Verify JSON validity after editing: jq . .claude/agents/agents-index.json > /dev/null

ROLLBACK: git checkout .claude/agents/agents-index.json

These are leaf agents spawned by team-run only, matching the existing code reviewer pattern (backend-reviewer, frontend-reviewer, etc. are NOT on the allowlist). tasks.subject: Validation gate — verify all integration points
tasks.description:
Perform comprehensive validation of all 63 integration points (9 per agent x 7 agents) plus 5 shared resources = 68 total.

Validation checks (23 total — expanded from v1's 20 to cover pasteur and C-1 fix):

1. FILE EXISTENCE: All 7 agent .md files exist at .claude/agents/{id}/{id}.md (genomics-reviewer, proteomics-reviewer, proteogenomics-reviewer, proteoform-reviewer, mass-spec-reviewer, bioinformatician-reviewer, pasteur)
2. FILE EXISTENCE: All 7 sharp-edges.yaml files exist at .claude/agents/{id}/sharp-edges.yaml
3. FILE EXISTENCE: .claude/schemas/teams/review-bioinformatics.json exists
4. FILE EXISTENCE: .claude/schemas/stdin/bioinformatics-reviewer.json exists
5. FILE EXISTENCE: .claude/schemas/stdin/bioinformatics-pasteur.json exists
6. FILE EXISTENCE: .claude/skills/review-bioinformatics/SKILL.md exists
7. JSON VALIDITY: jq . .claude/agents/agents-index.json (must exit 0)
8. JSON VALIDITY: jq . .claude/routing-schema.json (must exit 0)
9. JSON VALIDITY: jq . .claude/schemas/teams/review-bioinformatics.json (must exit 0)
10. JSON VALIDITY: jq . .claude/schemas/stdin/bioinformatics-reviewer.json (must exit 0)
11. JSON VALIDITY: jq . .claude/schemas/stdin/bioinformatics-pasteur.json (must exit 0)
12. ID CONSISTENCY: For each of 7 agents, id in frontmatter matches directory name
13. SUBAGENT_TYPE CONSISTENCY: For each of 7 agents, subagent_type in frontmatter matches agent_subagent_mapping value in routing-schema.json AND subagent_type in agents-index.json (3-way match)
14. AGENTS-INDEX COMPLETENESS: All 7 agent IDs appear in agents-index.json agents[] array
15. ROUTING-SCHEMA agent_subagent_mapping: All 7 mappings present
16. ROUTING-SCHEMA tiers.opus.agents: All 7 agent IDs present
17. C-1 VERIFICATION: tiers.opus.task_invocation_allowlist does NOT contain any of the 7 new agent IDs (grep for each ID in allowlist — all must return 0 matches)
18. ROUTING-SCHEMA subagent_types: bioinformatics_review category exists
19. ROUTING_RULES model_tiers.opus: All 7 agent IDs in agents-index.json routing_rules.model_tiers.opus
20. CLAUDE.MD DISPATCH: All 7 agents appear in CLAUDE.md dispatch table
21. CLAUDE.MD SLASH: '/review-bioinformatics' appears in CLAUDE.md slash commands table
22. TEAM CONFIG: review-bioinformatics.json has 2 waves, wave 0 has 6 members, wave 1 has 1 member (pasteur)
23. NO DEAD FIELDS: Grep all 7 agent .md files for forbidden fields: max_tokens, context_window, interleaved_thinking, compaction, structured_outputs, fast_mode (all must return 0 matches)

Output format:
```
=== Validation Report ===
Check  1: FILE_EXIST agent .md files ............. [PASS/FAIL]
Check  2: FILE_EXIST sharp-edges.yaml ............ [PASS/FAIL]
...
Check 23: NO_DEAD_FIELDS ......................... [PASS/FAIL]

Total: XX/23 PASS | XX/23 FAIL
Overall: [PASS/FAIL]
```

If ANY check fails, include:
- Which specific items failed
- Rollback instruction for that category

Rollback guide:
- Agent files: Delete directory .claude/agents/{id}/
- agents-index.json: git checkout .claude/agents/agents-index.json
- routing-schema.json: git checkout .claude/routing-schema.json
- CLAUDE.md: git checkout .claude/CLAUDE.md
- Team config: Delete .claude/schemas/teams/review-bioinformatics.json
- Stdin schemas: Delete .claude/schemas/stdin/bioinformatics-reviewer.json and bioinformatics-pasteur.json
- Skill: Delete directory .claude/skills/review-bioinformatics/
